Nephritis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the kidney.
Nephritis.Grade 1: Creatinine level increase of >0.3 mg/dL; creatinine 1.5-2.0 × baseline
Nephritis.Grade 3: Creatinine >3 × baseline or >4.0 mg/dL; hospitalization indicated

Acute kidney injury.Definition: A disorder characterized by the acute loss of renal function and/or a reduction in urine output.
Acute kidney injury.Grade 1: Creatinine level increase of >0.3 mg/dL; creatinine 1.5-2.0 × baseline
Acute kidney injury.Grade 3: Creatinine >3 × baseline or >4.0 mg/dL; hospitalization indicated

Proteinuria.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate the presence of excessive protein in the urine.
Proteinuria.Grade 3: Urinary protein >3.5 g/24 hrs; hospitalization indicated

Renal and urinary disorders - Other.Definition: A disorder characterized by a pathological process of the renal or urinary system.
Renal and urinary disorders - Other.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Renal and urinary disorders - Other.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Renal and urinary disorders - Other.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL
